surface.name: Send and receive a first Twilio SMS with Python and Virtual Phone
surface.selection_basis: Workshop selection policy (2026-07-19): when official docs present peer routes, commit to the fastest and most commonly used documented route. Prefer the vendor's first-listed or recommended quickstart, dashboard/console over CLI when both are peers, and the route with an explicit vendor time claim or fewest documented steps when tied. Selected route: Send and receive a first Twilio SMS with Python and Virtual Phone. The official Messaging quickstart provides a testable trial route that ends with an SMS received on Twilio's Virtual Phone, avoiding an inferred real-recipient configuration. Prior surface before policy commit: Common trial-account path, unresolved at product quickstart selection.

alternatives_considered.name: Programmable Messaging quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: General Usage lists Messaging as one of three equal product choices.

alternatives_considered.name: Programmable Voice quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: General Usage lists Voice as a peer choice rather than a universal route.

alternatives_considered.name: Verify quickstarts
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Verify has separate language and channel branches and is a peer product choice.

alternatives_considered.name: No-code SMS with Twilio Studio
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is a no-code Messaging path, not a cross-product default.

alternatives_considered.name: SendGrid Email
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The trial Console also exposes Email, but the general Twilio usage page does not select it as the universal first path.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create the common Twilio trial account, reach the Console, then choose the communications product whose first success should be measured.
assumed_prior_state: The developer does not yet have a Twilio account.
No channel or product intent has been supplied for this company-level roster entry.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: The selected SMS route demonstrates first success when the expected SMS text is visible as a received message in Virtual Phone.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The expected SMS text is visible as a received message.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The expected SMS text is visible as a received message.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The expected SMS text is visible as a received message.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: Selected under workshop fastest/most-used policy; terminal taken from the committed official candidate route.

prerequisites.requirement: A Twilio free trial can be created without a credit card. It expires after 30 days and uses product-specific free units, such as 100 SMS messages, 3,000 emails, and 75 voice minutes, rather than a shared dollar balance.
An email address and a personal phone number that Twilio can verify.
A selected Twilio product or channel before a product-level first-success journey can continue.

primary_path.action: Open Twilio Docs and follow Get started with Twilio essentials or General Usage.
details: The docs home presents many communications, authentication, customer-data, and builder products.
primary_path.success_signal: Get started with Twilio and trial-account guidance are visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: None documented.

primary_path.action: Start a Twilio free-trial account registration.
details: Twilio states that no credit card is required.
The trial expires after 30 days and includes product-specific free units.
primary_path.input: Email identity and personal phone number.
primary_path.success_signal: The account verification flow begins.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Trial availability and supported countries can constrain registration.

primary_path.action: Verify the email address.
primary_path.success_signal: The registration flow accepts the email verification.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing or expired email verification blocks progression; retry details are not given.

primary_path.action: Verify the personal phone number.
primary_path.input: A reachable personal phone number and its verification challenge.
primary_path.success_signal: Twilio accepts the phone verification.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Unsupported geography or inability to receive the challenge blocks the trial.

primary_path.action: Customize the account with project information.
details: The guide does not enumerate the current project-question fields.
primary_path.input: Answers describing the intended project or product.
primary_path.success_signal: The signup flow completes.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Product selection here changes the Console home options.

primary_path.action: Open the product-adapted Twilio Console home.
details: The Console exposes Account SID and Auth Token, usage, product navigation, and product-specific try-out paths.
primary_path.success_signal: The developer can see credentials and product options.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: None documented after successful signup.

primary_path.action: Choose the product first-success route.
details: General Usage names Programmable SMS, Programmable Voice, and 2FA with Verify.
The Console can also expose SMS, Voice, Email, and WhatsApp try-outs.
primary_path.success_signal: A guide with a channel-specific terminal success is selected.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The supplied roster entry does not identify the product, so research stops rather than inferring one.

primary_path.action: Start a Twilio free-trial registration and choose Continue with trial when prompted for a plan.
details: Twilio states that the trial requires no credit card and expires after 30 days.
primary_path.input: An email identity and a personal phone number.
primary_path.success_signal: The registration advances to identity verification.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Trial availability and supported geography can constrain registration; choosing a paid plan changes this route.

primary_path.action: Open Twilio's verification email and verify the account email address.
details: Email verification is part of the documented trial signup.
primary_path.success_signal: Twilio accepts the email verification.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A missing or expired message blocks signup; the guide does not give a delivery time.

primary_path.action: Complete the verification challenge for the personal phone number.
details: The trial uses a personal phone number as an additional verification gate.
primary_path.input: A reachable supported phone number and the delivered challenge.
primary_path.success_signal: Twilio accepts the phone verification.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Unsupported geography or inability to receive the challenge blocks the trial.

primary_path.action: Answer the project-customization questions and complete signup.
details: Twilio adapts the Console home to the selected project information, but the guide does not enumerate the current fields.
primary_path.input: Answers describing the intended Messaging project.
primary_path.success_signal: The Twilio Console home opens.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The questions can alter the Console presentation; their current field-level form is not documented.

primary_path.action: Confirm Python 3.8 through 3.13, install ngrok, then install Flask and the Twilio SDK with pip install flask twilio.
details: The Messaging quickstart lists Python 3.8 through 3.13, ngrok, Flask, and the Twilio Python SDK as prerequisites.
primary_path.input: A supported Python and pip environment plus the documented ngrok installation route.
primary_path.output: ngrok plus the Flask and Twilio Python packages in the development environment.
primary_path.success_signal: ngrok is available and pip installs both Flask and Twilio without error.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An unsupported runtime or failure to install ngrok, Flask, or Twilio leaves the documented prerequisite set incomplete.

primary_path.action: Obtain an SMS-capable Twilio phone number from the Console.
details: Use Get a phone number on the dashboard or Phone Numbers, Manage, Buy a number.
primary_path.success_signal: The number appears in the account and can be selected by the Messaging quickstart.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Number availability and capabilities vary; a number without SMS capability cannot send this message.

primary_path.action: Copy the Account SID and Auth Token from the Twilio Console.
details: These credentials authenticate the Python client.
primary_path.output: The account SID and authentication token.
primary_path.success_signal: Both credential values are available for local configuration.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing or incorrect credentials cause authentication failure when the script runs.

primary_path.action: Store the SID and token as TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID and TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN environment variables.
details: The guide recommends environment variables instead of hard-coded credentials.
primary_path.output: Credential environment variables in the shell used for the sample.
primary_path.success_signal: The Python process can read both variables.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Unset or stale variables cause client authentication failure.

primary_path.action: Open the Send an SMS Console page, select Send to Virtual Phone, choose the Twilio number, and open Virtual Phone.
details: The Virtual Phone supplies the documented test destination +18777804236.
primary_path.output: An open Virtual Phone destination ready to display the test SMS.
primary_path.success_signal: Virtual Phone is visible and the sender number is selected.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Without a selected SMS-capable number, the documented send configuration is incomplete.

primary_path.action: Create send_sms.py with the documented Twilio Client and messages.create call.
details: Read the SID and token from the environment, set the message body, use the Twilio number as from_, and use +18777804236 as to.
primary_path.input: The official Python sample, sender number, and Virtual Phone destination.
primary_path.success_signal: The sample contains the authenticated client and complete message parameters.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A wrong sender, destination, or credential reference prevents delivery.

primary_path.action: Run python send_sms.py.
details: The script asks Twilio Messaging to create and send the SMS.
primary_path.input: The configured script, credentials, and network access.
primary_path.output: A created message request or a Twilio error.
primary_path.success_signal: The command completes without an authentication or request error.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Trial restrictions, invalid credentials, an incapable sender, or malformed message parameters can reject the request.

primary_path.action: Process and deliver the SMS to Virtual Phone.
details: The quickstart says delivery may take a few moments.
primary_path.output: A message delivered to the Virtual Phone interface.
primary_path.success_signal: The new SMS appears in Virtual Phone.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Delivery is an explicit wait; absence of the message means the terminal success is not yet established.

primary_path.action: Verify that Virtual Phone displays the SMS body sent by the Python script.
details: Receiving the expected content is the observable end-to-end result.
primary_path.output: A confirmed first Twilio SMS delivery.
primary_path.success_signal: The expected SMS text is visible as a received message.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A missing or different message does not satisfy first success.

branches.condition: The intended outcome is a first SMS.
branches.path: Choose Programmable Messaging and research its language or no-code route, sender/number acquisition, recipient verification, regulatory gates, request, and delivery signal.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Defines success around an SMS and introduces phone-number, geography, and trial-message restrictions.

branches.condition: The intended outcome is a first voice call.
branches.path: Choose Programmable Voice and research its phone-number, code/TwiML, call request, and call-status path.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes the execution and observable signal from a message to a call.

branches.condition: The intended outcome is user verification.
branches.path: Choose Verify, select SMS, WhatsApp, Voice, Email, TOTP, or Push plus a language, then follow token-send and validation steps.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds service configuration, channel, token, and validation actions.

branches.condition: The intended product is Email, WhatsApp, Video, Conversations, Segment, or another Twilio surface.
branches.path: Open that product's dedicated getting-started guide and establish its terminal state independently.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Produces a materially different journey and boundary.

friction_gates.description: A Twilio trial account is required for the common self-service route; no credit card is required to start it.
The trial expires after 30 days and grants product-specific free units instead of a shared dollar balance, so the selected product determines the usable allowance.
Signup requires a reachable personal phone number and verification.
Project/product answers customize the Console and can steer later onboarding.
Twilio requires a product/channel choice before a meaningful first success exists.
Trial product paths can have geography, verified-recipient, content, daily-message, phone-number, and regulatory restrictions.

sources.title: Twilio Docs
evidence_supported: No single product selected on the home page

sources.title: General Usage
evidence_supported: Instruction to choose SMS, Voice, or Verify

sources.title: Get started with your Twilio free trial account
sections_used: Explore Twilio from your Console home page
evidence_supported: 30-day trial expiration and product-specific free-unit examples

sources.title: Free Trial Account Restrictions and Limitations
evidence_supported: Verified-recipient, daily-message, sender, and account restrictions affecting Messaging candidate

sources.title: Messaging Quickstart
sections_used: Sign up for Twilio and get a phone number
evidence_supported: Virtual Phone destination and received-message verification

uncertainties.question: Which Twilio product and channel should the workshop measure?
uncertainties.impact: It changes every post-account step and the first-success signal.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Official getting-started guidance explicitly asks the developer to choose a product, while the roster supplies only the company.

uncertainties.question: Which current project questions appear during trial-account customization?
uncertainties.impact: The common signup path cannot be decomposed field by field.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The trial guide says to provide project information without enumerating the current fields.

excluded_after_success: No product-specific downstream actions can be excluded until the Twilio product boundary is chosen.
